question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSSUBMITMESSAGE transaction via blockstream with an empty topicRunningHash, causing Utility.handleRecoverableError() to silently skip the message, so that a downstream fund-release instruction encoded in that topic message is permanently lost and the associated funds are frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a fragmented CONSENSUSSUBMITMESSAGE where chunkInfo.getNumber() equals chunkInfo.getTotal() but earlier chunks were silently skipped via the blockstream empty-runningHash path, causing the mirror node to store an incomplete chunk sequence that downstream fund-release smart contracts can never fully reassemble, permanently freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSSUBMITMESSAGE with chunkInfo.getTotal() set to an arbitrarily large integer (e.g., Integer.MAX_VALUE) while only submitting one chunk, causing the mirror node to store a TopicMessage with chunkTotal=MAX_VALUE and chunkNum=1 that downstream fund-release consumers wait on indefinitely, permanently freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSSUBMITMESSAGE with receipt.getTopicRunningHashVersion() equal to DEFAULT_RUNNING_HASH_VERSION (3), causing the handler to skip setRunningHashVersion() entirely (null stored), so that downstream consumers that require a non-null runningHashVersion to verify fund-release messages fail permanently?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CONSENSUSSUBMITMESSAGE with a receipt.getTopicSequenceNumber() that is non-sequential (e.g., skipping sequence numbers), causing the mirror node to store a TopicMessage with an incorrect sequenceNumber that breaks the sequence-number ordering invariant relied upon by fund-release consumers, permanently freezing funds?
